# SheetJS Community Edition - Licensing

## Summary
SheetJS Community Edition is licensed under Apache 2.0. The library remains free and open source. A commercial "Professional Edition" exists with enhanced features, but the Community Edition is genuinely free for commercial use with attribution requirements.

## Key quotations / statistics
- Community Edition: Apache 2.0 License - commercial use permitted, attribution required
- "truly open source" (from README)
- Attribution: must include specified attribution text in open source disclosures
- Professional Edition: enhanced features, broad compatibility, priority support (paid)
- No announced licensing changes for 2026

## Annotations for weapon-forge
- Directly answers Command Brief Q2: SheetJS Community Edition remained Apache 2.0 in 2026 - no commercial-only gate for the core parse/write functionality
- SheetJS Pro is worth noting: Pro adds advanced streaming and better compatibility, but is not required for standard CSV/XLSX parse use cases
- Attribution requirement is often overlooked - add a note in the skill about adding attribution to OSS disclosures
